commands.review_summary:
The PR preserves deferred MCP/LSP selectors when effective parent tool allowlists are inherited by spawned sessions and filters those selectors through the applicable policy layers.

PR surface: Source +143, Tests +165. Total +308 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level with high confidence: current main replaces the inherited allowlist with concrete parent tool names, deterministically dropping selectors for tools materialized only in the spawned child. The linked issue also supplies exported trajectory evidence, though this read-only review did not execute the reproduction.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] No definite code repair remains; maintainers should wait for contributor-owned real `sessions_spawn` proof before deciding to merge.

target.title: fix(agents): preserve runtime tool inheritance tokens
target.body:
## Summary
- Preserve runtime-materialized allowlist tokens when spawned sessions inherit an effective parent tool surface.
- Filter deferred MCP/LSP selectors through the same effective profile/agent/group/subagent/inherited policy layers before writing child inherited allowlists, so broad `bundle-mcp`/`group:plugins` entries cannot bypass narrower policies.
- Cover the shared helper, gateway resolution, and embedded tool construction paths, including broad-allow then narrow-allow/deny regressions.

Refs #85030

## Verification
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/agents/tool-policy.ts src/gateway/tool-resolution.ts src/agents/agent-tools.ts src/agents/tool-policy.test.ts src/gateway/tool-resolution.exclude.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tool-policy.test.ts src/gateway/tool-resolution.exclude.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm test:docker:cron-mcp-cleanup`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Spawned subagent inherited allowlists dropped runtime materialization policy tokens such as `bundle-mcp`, `group:plugins`, MCP `server__tool` names, and `lsp_` tool names when the parent effective tool surface did not already contain materialized runtime tools; preserving those tokens must also respect narrower effective policy layers.
Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS, Docker 29.4.0, repository Docker E2E image built from this branch, real Gateway process, mock OpenAI-compatible server, and real stdio MCP probe servers. The focused MCP invocation proof used the production `createSessionMcpRuntime`, `materializeBundleMcpToolsForRun`, and child attempt allowlist filtering modules against a temporary stdio MCP server.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: After commit `419086e4c141d03874d7bec1730c099a96a84b55`, ran `corepack pnpm test:docker:cron-mcp-cleanup`. Then ran a temporary `corepack pnpm exec tsx` proof script that started a stdio MCP server with `allowed` and `blocked` tools, materialized both tools for `sessionKey: "agent:main:subagent:proof-child"`, applied a child inherited allowlist of `read`, `sessions_spawn`, and `probe__allowed`, and invoked the surviving MCP tool.
Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the focused MCP invocation proof:

```json
{
  "materializedToolNames": [
    "probe__allowed",
    "probe__blocked"
  ],
  "childInheritedToolsAllow": [
    "read",
    "sessions_spawn",
    "probe__allowed"
  ],
  "childVisibleMcpToolNames": [
    "probe__allowed"
  ],
  "blockedVisible": false,
  "invocationResult": {
    "content": [
      {
        "type": "text",
        "text": "subagent-mcp-allowed-ok"
      }
    ],
    "details": {
      "mcpServer": "probe",
      "mcpTool": "allowed"
    }
  }
}
```

Additional real Docker output from `corepack pnpm test:docker:cron-mcp-cleanup`:

```text
OpenClaw package tarball integrity passed.
Running in-container cron/subagent MCP cleanup smoke...
{"ok":true,"cron":{"jobId":"ed353e39-08bc-408e-8867-6a99df36a679","runId":"manual:ed353e39-08bc-408e-8867-6a99df36a679:1781294149608:1","pid":282,"status":"ok"},"subagent":{"runId":"d6a7af12-ed53-416a-b917-8f0c234d2f14","exitedPids":[295],"pids":[295]}}
OK
```

Observed result after fix: The production child-runtime proof materialized both MCP tools from the real stdio server, exposed only the selector that survived the child inherited allowlist (`probe__allowed`), kept the conflicting selector absent (`blockedVisible: false`), and successfully invoked the allowed MCP tool. The Docker smoke also accepted and completed a one-shot subagent run while its seeded stdio MCP probe process started and was cleaned up after the run.
What was not tested: A credentialed external MCP server or a live paid LLM provider. The proof uses local stdio MCP servers and the repository mock OpenAI-compatible server for deterministic behavior.
target.author: yu-xin-c
